answer.en: HTML encoding converts special characters to HTML entities so they are displayed as text rather than interpreted as HTML markup. For example, < becomes < and & becomes &.
HTML encoding is critical for security. Displaying user-supplied text without encoding can lead to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks. Always encode untrusted input before inserting it into HTML.

question.en: How do I decode HTML entities?

answer.en: Use the HTML Decoder tool to convert HTML entities back to their original characters.
